project.name: solocorn-app
project.description: TutorMekimi (Solocorn) is an AI-powered tutoring platform built with Next.js App Router, Zustand state management, Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL, and an ADK AI service backend. Features include real-time video sessions, whiteboard collaboration, course builder, assessment tools, and multi-role dashboards for students, tutors, and parents.

layers.name: Data Layer

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: Docker containers, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions CI/CD, project configuration files, build and deployment scripts, public assets, devcontainer setup, and deployment automation for both the Next.js app and ADK service.

layers.name: Testing
layers.description: Vitest unit and integration tests, Playwright E2E tests, security test suites, accessibility tests, and test helper utilities covering auth, API, components, and business logic behavior.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Next.js App Router API route handlers and API utility modules that process HTTP requests, validate parameters, handle errors, and orchestrate responses for REST endpoints across all user roles.

layers.name: Frontend UI
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages, React components, custom hooks, Zustand state stores, Tailwind styles, type definitions, and the landing page — all user-facing presentation code for tutor, student, parent, and admin portals.

layers.name: AI Service Layer
layers.description: AI tutoring engine including OpenAI SDK integrations, agent orchestration, prompt management, teaching strategies, memory stores, and the standalone ADK Python service for live transcription and AI tutoring.

layers.name: Auth & Security
layers.description: NextAuth.js authentication, RBAC authorization, CSRF protection, rate limiting, input sanitization, file upload security, payment security, and edge middleware for request security and access control.

layers.name: Business Logic
layers.description: Core domain services including course management, scheduling, enrollment, payments, notifications, i18n, monitoring, Sentry observability, and shared utilities that implement the tutoring platform business rules.

layers.name: Real-time Services

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand TutorMekimi's purpose as an AI-powered tutoring platform. This document outlines the system architecture: a Next.js App Router frontend with Zustand state management, Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL for data persistence, and an ADK AI service backend for intelligent tutoring. It provides the roadmap for how all the pieces fit together.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The root layout.tsx is the Next.js App Router entry point that wraps every page in the application. It sets up the foundational HTML structure, metadata, and imports the Providers component that initializes global context. This is where the application bootstraps its rendering tree before any page-specific code runs.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router uses layout.tsx files to define shared UI that persists across navigation. The root layout at src/app/layout.tsx wraps the entire application, while nested layouts in subdirectories provide scoped wrappers for specific sections like admin or tutor dashboards.

tour.title: Database Layer
tour.description: The database layer is the foundation of the entire platform. drizzle.ts configures the Drizzle ORM connection to PostgreSQL, while the schema definitions in tables/ establish the domain model for users, courses, classrooms, content, and more. The schema index.ts acts as a barrel file that centralizes all table exports, making it the most imported file in the codebase with 173 dependents.
tour.languageLesson: Drizzle ORM uses a type-safe schema-first approach where table definitions are written as TypeScript objects. The pgTable helper defines columns with types like varchar, integer, and timestamp, while relations() establishes foreign key relationships. This schema code generates both TypeScript types and SQL migrations.

tour.title: Authentication & Security
tour.description: Authentication is handled through NextAuth.js with the [...nextauth] catch-all route, while the core auth.ts module implements session management, JWT handling, and role-based access control. The middleware.ts file provides API route protection with 150 dependent routes, handling authentication checks, rate limiting, and CSRF protection uniformly across the API surface.
tour.languageLesson: NextAuth.js in App Router uses a catch-all route pattern [...nextauth] that handles all authentication endpoints under /api/auth/. The auth.ts configuration defines providers, callbacks, and session strategies. Middleware wraps API routes to enforce authentication before business logic executes.

tour.title: State Management
tour.description: Zustand stores manage client-side state across the application. The communication store handles messaging between tutors and students, while the live-class store manages real-time classroom state. These stores are used by dashboard components, chat interfaces, and live session UIs to maintain synchronized state without prop drilling through deep component trees.
tour.languageLesson: Zustand is a minimal state management library that uses hooks. Stores are created with create() and expose actions as plain functions. Unlike Redux, Zustand requires no providers or reducers, and state slices can be composed by importing and calling other stores' actions directly.

tour.title: UI Component System
tour.description: The UI layer is built on a comprehensive design system of reusable components. button.tsx is the most imported file in the entire codebase (218 dependents), followed by card.tsx and badge.tsx. These shadcn/ui-based components provide consistent styling, accessibility, and behavior across all user-facing pages. They form the visual vocabulary that every feature builds upon.
tour.languageLesson: shadcn/ui components are built on Radix UI primitives for accessibility and styled with Tailwind CSS. The cn() utility from utils.ts merges Tailwind classes using clsx and tailwind-merge, handling conditional classes and resolving conflicting utility classes automatically.

tour.title: AI Tutoring Engine
tour.description: The AI layer powers intelligent tutoring through multiple specialized agents. The tutor agent handles subject-specific teaching, the content generator creates quizzes and materials, the briefing agent prepares session plans, and the live monitor tracks classroom engagement. These agents use OpenAI's API with carefully crafted prompts to provide personalized educational experiences.
tour.languageLesson: The AI agent pattern here uses modular prompt engineering: each agent has an index.ts that orchestrates calls to LLM APIs with subject-specific prompt templates. The prompt-builder.ts pattern separates prompt construction from API execution, allowing prompts to be composed, tested, and versioned independently of the calling code.

tour.title: API Routes
tour.description: The API layer consists of 180 Next.js App Router route handlers organized by domain. The AI routes handle tutor chat, content generation, and monitoring. The auth routes manage registration, sessions, and password flows. The class routes power real-time classroom features. Each route follows the standard Next.js pattern of exporting GET, POST, PUT, or DELETE handlers that return JSON responses.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router API routes use file-based routing where each route.ts exports HTTP method handlers. Dynamic segments like [id] create parameterized routes. The route handlers receive NextRequest objects and return NextResponse.json() for type-safe JSON responses. This replaces the older pages/api pattern with a more integrated approach.

tour.title: Real-time Services
tour.description: Real-time communication powers the live classroom experience. The socket-server-enhanced.ts manages Socket.IO connections for chat, polls, and whiteboard collaboration. The socket-state.ts module tracks room state, user presence, and message history. The use-socket.ts hook provides React components with a clean interface to send and receive real-time events.
tour.languageLesson: Socket.IO enables bidirectional event-based communication over WebSockets with automatic fallback to HTTP long-polling. The server maintains room state keyed by session IDs, while the React hook useSocket encapsulates connection lifecycle, event subscription cleanup, and reconnection handling in a reusable pattern.

tour.title: Live Classroom
tour.description: The live classroom is the platform's core interactive feature. The enhanced-whiteboard.tsx provides a collaborative drawing surface with tool menus, LaTeX formula support, and graph plotting. The video-player integrates Daily.co for video conferencing. The quick-poll.tsx enables tutors to create instant polls. Together these components create a rich synchronous learning environment.
tour.languageLesson: The whiteboard uses a canvas-based rendering approach with custom tool implementations for drawing, text, shapes, and formulas. State is synchronized across clients through Socket.IO delta messages that transmit only changed strokes rather than full canvas bitmaps, minimizing bandwidth during real-time collaboration.

tour.title: Business Logic Services
tour.description: Core domain services implement the platform's tutoring business rules. The course-builder.service.ts manages course creation and variant scheduling. The feedback workflow handles tutor-student assessment loops. The registration service orchestrates user onboarding with role-specific flows for students, tutors, and parents. These services sit between the API routes and the database, encapsulating complex multi-step operations.

tour.title: Database Migrations
tour.description: The Drizzle migration files in the drizzle/ directory track the evolution of the PostgreSQL schema across 50+ incremental changes. Each migration file is named with a sequential number and describes the schema change it applies, from initial table creation through feature additions like whiteboard versioning, curriculum categories, and course scheduling. The migration journal tracks which migrations have been applied.
tour.languageLesson: Database migrations should be ordered and idempotent. Drizzle generates migration SQL from schema changes, and the _journal.json tracks applied migrations to prevent re-execution. Each migration file should only move forward (additive changes or separate destructive cleanup migrations) to support zero-downtime deployments.

tour.title: Containerization & Deployment
tour.description: The Dockerfile packages the Next.js application into a production-ready container image for GCP Cloud Run deployment. The docker-compose.prod.yml defines multi-service orchestration including the PostgreSQL database and ADK AI service. The GitHub Actions workflow in deploy-gcp.yml automates the CI/CD pipeline, building and deploying on every push to main.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use multiple FROM statements to separate build and runtime environments. The builder stage installs dev dependencies and compiles the application, while the final stage copies only the production artifacts and runtime dependencies. This pattern reduces the final image size by 50-80% and minimizes the attack surface by excluding build tools from production.
